Title: Pathway-specific enzymes from bamboo and crop leaves biosynthesize anti-nociceptive C-glycosylated flavones

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: C-glucosylated 2-hydroxynaringenin intermediates; nothofagin; vitexin/isovitexin after dehydration
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to 2-hydroxylated flavanone intermediates or phloretin through C-glycosidic bond formation.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream modification step after F2H; branch-specific glycosylation step
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms C-glycosylated intermediates that dehydrate to target CGFs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anti-nociceptive and anti-inflammatory flavones; isoorientin showed neuroanalgesic and anti-inflammatory activity
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in E. coli fermentation strains
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaf tissue accumulation survey in grasses; heterologous fermentation in minimal medium with glucose and tyrosine or naringenin feeding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative genomics; cloning; recombinant protein expression in E. coli; in vitro enzyme assay with UDP-Glc/UDP-Ara and acceptors; LC-MS/MS; HPLC; NMR; kinetic analysis; heterologous pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PhF2H; PhF3'H; AtCPR2 in engineered pathway context
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key pathway-specific C-glycosylation step required for CGF formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables biosynthesis of C-glucosylated flavones in vitro and in E. coli, supporting production of vitexin/isovitexin and, with hydroxylases, orientin/isoorientin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PhUGT708A43 from bamboo was identified as a potent clade A1 C-glucosyltransferase. 2. It showed high affinity and high catalytic efficiency toward 2-hydroxynaringenin. 3. In engineered E. coli, it supported production of vitexin/isovitexin and, combined with F2H/F3'H modules, orientin/isoorientin.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In E. coli, substantial accumulation of C-glycosylated 2-hydroxylated intermediates indicated incomplete dehydration to final flavones
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful pathway gene for microbial or plant metabolic engineering of anti-nociceptive CGFs; suitable for stacking with F2H and F3'H to produce desired flavone C-glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative genomics using Ensembl Plants; phylogenetic analysis; metabolite profiling by LC-HR-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: T7-based inducible expression in pET28a/pETDuet system
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reported in manuscript also as bamboo CGT ortholog and used as confirmative CGT module in cell factory

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates flavanone at C-2 to form unstable 2-hydroxylated intermediate used for C-glycosylation.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream pathway-specific decoration step before C-glycosylation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor for CGT-mediated C-glycoside formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in E. coli after codon optimization and N-terminal engineering
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum membrane in plants; engineered for bacterial expression
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: E. coli fermentation from glucose/tyrosine or naringenin feeding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; codon optimization; N-terminal truncation/2B1dH tagging; coexpression with AtCPR2 and PhUGT708A43; LC-MS metabolite detection in vivo
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: AtCPR2; PhUGT708A43; OsCYP75B3 or AtCYP75B1 in full pathway constructs
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key pathway-specific enzyme for entry into CGF branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports conversion of naringenin into 2-hydroxynaringenin and enables vitexin/isovitexin production in E. coli; bamboo enzyme accumulated high levels of C-glycosylated 2-hydroxynaringenin intermediates and gave strong CGF productivity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Bamboo PhF2H was identified as a previously unreported CYP93G2-like F2H. 2. Engineered PhF2H functioned efficiently in E. coli with AtCPR2 and PhUGT708A43. 3. Feeding naringenin to the PhF2H/PhUGT708A43 strain increased vitexin and isovitexin titers to 35.1 and 45.8 mg/L.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Accumulation of glycosylated 2-hydroxylated intermediates suggests incomplete downstream dehydration in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable pathway-specific hydroxylase for engineering microbial or plant CGF production, particularly with bamboo CGT
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative genomics; metabolite profiling by LC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: T7-based inducible expression in pETDuet system
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PhF2H and PmF2H were reported as completely identical proteins

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Eriodictyol; downstream orientin/isoorientin with F2H and CGT
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates the B-ring 3' position of naringenin to produce eriodictyol, enabling luteolin-type CGFs.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream decoration step supplying hydroxylated precursor for orientin/isoorientin formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for target luteolin-derived CGFs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anti-nociceptive and anti-inflammatory; isoorientin most potent in mouse assays
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in E. coli after codon optimization and N-terminal engineering
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum membrane in plants; engineered for bacterial expression
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: E. coli fermentation from glucose and tyrosine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Candidate screening in E. coli; codon optimization; N-terminal engineering; LC-MS detection of eriodictyol and final CGFs
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key precursor-supplying hydroxylase for luteolin-type CGF branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides 3'-hydroxylated precursor for orientin/isoorientin biosynthesis; bamboo Ph/PmF3'H showed 40.7% conversion of naringenin and was functionally active in pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among four bamboo F3'H-like genes, PH01001730G0080-encoded protein (Ph/PmF3'H) displayed substantial 3'-hydroxylase activity toward naringenin. 2. This enzyme enabled production of luteolin-derived CGFs in reconstructed pathways. 3. It was identified as a bamboo pathway gene contributing to orientin/isoorientin formation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower activity than top heterologous F3'H combinations may limit final titer
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering isoorientin/orientin production when stacked with F2H and CGT
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Candidate gene mining from bamboo genome; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: T7-based inducible expression in pETDuet system
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Ph/PmF3'H corresponds to PH01001730G0080 in P. heterocycla genome

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: C-glucosylated flavanone intermediates; nothofagin; vitexin/isovitexin after dehydration
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to 2-hydroxylated flavanone intermediates or phloretin through C-glycosylation.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms C-glycosylated intermediates leading to CGFs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves; hulls; grains in grasses generally
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Used as positive control in enzymatic assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Previously reported gene used as positive control; comparative genomics contextualization; in vitro assays in this study
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: OsCYP93G2 in rice CGF locus context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Served as validated positive control defining clade A1 CGT function and rice CGF biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsUGT708A3 was used as the known rice CGT benchmark. 2. Its activity matched products generated by newly identified clade A1 enzymes. 3. It anchors the syntenic CGT locus used for discovery of related genes.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reference CGT for engineering and comparative discovery of monocot CGTs
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Known CGF-biosynthesizing gene from prior work, included directly in Results as comparator and locus anchor

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates naringenin at C-2 to generate unstable 2-hydroxynaringenin for CGT action.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor for C-glycoside formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Engineered and expressed in E. coli with AtCPR2
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum membrane in plants; engineered for bacterial expression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Codon optimization; N-terminal modification; coexpression with AtCPR2 and PhUGT708A43; LC-MS product analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enabled production of vitexin/isovitexin in E. coli when paired with CGT
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCYP93G2 was one of the known crop F2Hs tested in the microbial platform. 2. Engineered enzyme catalyzed naringenin conversion to support CGF biosynthesis. 3. It validated the pathway design used to compare bamboo F2H activity.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Less accumulation of glycosylated 2-hydroxynaringenin than bamboo F2H strains
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful benchmark F2H for synthetic biology of CGFs
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Known rice CGF locus gene used in discovery pipeline

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates naringenin at C-2 for downstream C-glycosylation.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor for target CGFs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in E. coli with AtCPR2 and CGT module
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum membrane in plants; engineered for bacterial expression
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: E. coli fermentation from glucose and tyrosine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Codon optimization; N-terminal modification; pathway reconstruction; LC-MS/MS quantification
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: AtCPR2; PhUGT708A43; OsCYP75B3 in final best strain
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Among tested F2Hs, engineered ZmF2H showed near-complete naringenin conversion and high vitexin productivity; with OsCYP75B3 produced orientin/isoorientin in the best strain
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmCYP93G5 was highly effective for naringenin 2-hydroxylation in E. coli. 2. It supported the highest vitexin productivity among tested F2Hs. 3. Combined with OsCYP75B3 and PhUGT708A43, it enabled de novo production of orientin and isoorientin from glucose and tyrosine.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent engineering component for microbial CGF production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Text once refers to ZmCYP93G2 in figure description, but Methods specify ZmCYP93G5

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates naringenin at the 3' position to form eriodictyol for luteolin-type CGF biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum membrane in plants; engineered for bacterial expression
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: E. coli fermentation from glucose and tyrosine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Codon optimization; N-terminal engineering; pathway assembly with ZmF2H or PhF2H, AtCPR2, and PhUGT708A43; LC-MS/MS detection of final products
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Important precursor-supplying hydroxylase for luteolin-type CGFs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: In the best-performing full pathway strain, OsCYP75B3 enabled production of 26.6 mg/L isoorientin and 23.9 mg/L orientin without accumulation of C-glucosylated flavone intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCYP75B3 was one of the most active F3'H enzymes tested. 2. It was selected for complete P450 module assembly. 3. Together with ZmF2H, AtCPR2, and PhUGT708A43, it supported efficient de novo biosynthesis of orientin and isoorientin.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Highly valuable F3'H for engineering isoorientin/orientin production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OsCYP75B4 was inactive in this system and thus not core
